title: [Raichu] Battle Guide
summary: A fast [Electric] special [sweeper] that combines Speed 110 with [nasty-plot] setup — a self-sufficient cleaner that does not need light-ball to matter.
strengths: Unboosted Speed 110 outruns most of the upper metagame
weaknesses: Priority moves shut down the sweep attempt

recommendedMoves.reason: Pivot tempo move for bad matchups
Setup move that doubles SpA in one turn

recommendedItems.reason: Alternative that turns into a scarfer

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Raichu] is a frail but fast [Electric] special [sweeper] that stands on its own without light-ball. Speed 110 and [nasty-plot] setup make it a self-sufficient cleaner, and [lightning-rod] gives the team a redirector for [Electric] matchups. Its role is simple: find one safe setup turn, then sweep with boosted [thunderbolt].

## Strengths
110 unboosted Speed sits above most of the upper metagame, so [Raichu] rarely loses the race unboosted. [nasty-plot] is the key move — one use doubles SpA and turns [thunderbolt] into a top-tier special attack. [focus-blast] handles the [Steel], [Rock], and [Normal] walls that would otherwise wall the [Electric] STAB. [volt-switch] keeps pivot tempo alive for bad matchups, and [lightning-rod] supports teammates by absorbing [Electric] attacks.

## Weaknesses & Answers
60/55/80 bulk leaves very little setup room — any priority or 2x hit threatens a KO. [Ground] is a hard 2x weakness and [earthquake] users force [Raichu] out immediately. Speed 110 is outrun by most scarfed attackers, so the setup turn must be earned on a predicted switch or safe matchup. Priority moves like [extreme-speed] and [sucker-punch] can end the sweep before it starts.

## Recommended Moveset
[thunderbolt] is the locked STAB because consistency matters more than the damage ceiling. [focus-blast] is the [Fighting] coverage for [Steel], [Rock], and [Normal] targets. [volt-switch] is the pivot move for bad matchups, and the last slot is [nasty-plot] for the setup win condition. Support variants with [encore] or [knock-off] exist but sacrifice cleaning power.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the default item because it guarantees one safe setup turn. [choice-scarf] is the alternative that drops setup in favour of raw Speed and locks [Raichu] into a [revenge] role. [magnet] is the pure damage pick, boosting [thunderbolt] without changing the set fundamentals. Keep [lightning-rod] as the ability. Tera [Electric] maxes STAB damage while Tera [Fairy] flips [Dragon], [Dark], and [Fighting] weaknesses.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Raichu] wants [fake-out] leads or pivots that force opposing switches for free setup turns. A [Ground] answer — [Grass] or [Flying] partner — covers the [earthquake] weakness, and a cleaner behind [Raichu] converts the sweep chip into final KOs. [stealth-rock] support widens the OHKO ranges after a [nasty-plot] boost.

## Play Patterns
Find a safe turn to [nasty-plot], then open with [thunderbolt] on neutral matchups and [focus-blast] on [Steel] or [Normal] switch-ins. Use [volt-switch] to bail when the matchup is bad, and Tera on any telegraphed [Ground] move. Do not commit to the sweep unless priority is already handled.
